meaning: n.漆,漆,漆器vt.用漆涂于...,使表面光泽
examples: We put on the second coating of lacquer.
The decor is simple – black lacquer panels on white walls.
Even lacquer, which is a more uncertain market than either porcelain or jade, went as a swing.
Horowitz contracted a private company to cut lacquer disc recordings of all of his Carnegie Hall recitals.
Instead, yellow flowers daubed with black lacquer to resemble black-eyed Susans are substituted.
